summary: All three layers (backend/db/L1) reported pass and CRITICAL-2 (colluding_count derived from signing_queue) is verified, but bug-hunter surfaced two HIGH findings that materially undermine confidence: the L1 tx_hash '0x_l1slash_tx_abcd' looks like a stub literal (suggesting the L1 verification is a false-positive), and colluding_count can undercount under mid-flow status mutation. The pending_retry / fail-hard path was not exercised and L1 verification only reads getProverCount() rather than asserting on-chain slashed state, so the green run does not yet prove end-to-end correctness. Verdict is FIXABLE: tighten test infra (assert real tx_hash format + on-chain slashed flag + 60/20/20 recipients) and lock the colluding_count snapshot before treating this as a true PASS.
must_fix_before_merge: L1 tx_hash appears to be stub literal — assert real 0x[64hex] and on-chain slashed state
colluding_count race: snapshot signing_queue atomically before status mutation
Exercise PendingRetry / fail-hard path (not covered in this run)
L1 verification must check slashed flag, not just getProverCount()
unresolved_questions: Is '0x_l1slash_tx_abcd' produced by a mock L1 client or by the real submission path? If mock, the L1 layer pass is invalid and verdict should escalate to BLOCKED.
Does the SlashingRetryService have any test coverage at all, or is pending_retry purely theoretical in this run?
patches_applied_in_followup_commit: P1: count_signed_provers_for_lock filters on signed_at IS NOT NULL (append-only evidence) instead of mutable status='signed'
P2: update_slashing_l1_status validates canonical 0x[64 hex] format; stub literals rewritten to pending_retry with explanatory l1_error
